selection: Fermi estimates for every pro/contra argument of the RSI / overstimulation / zukunft.com discussion, with reproducible calc-validation chains
description: Encodes the pro and contra arguments of the thread into the zukunft.com graph. Each value carries a provenance qualifier triple - 'measured value' for figures taken from a source, 'assumed value' for set Fermi inputs - so that a set assumption can never be mistaken for a measurement (the TODO agreed in the discussion). Every calc-validation entry is reproduced by check.py and is order-independent of the input values.

words.name: measured
words.description: provenance qualifier: the number is taken from a cited source and is in principle verifiable

words.name: assumed
words.description: provenance qualifier: the number is a set Fermi input, not a measurement; the conclusion may hinge on it

words.name: status
words.description: as in status quo: the present-day un-intervened state

words.name: rollback
words.description: reduction of digital overstimulation, e.g. via content regulation / press-code enforcement

words.name: measurement
words.description: the act of running a study to estimate the sign-determining factor

words.name: neonatal
words.description: during the critical developmental window; the mouse study used p10 pups

words.name: readiness
words.description: willingness to subject oneself to the effort of checking, as opposed to the ability to do so

words.name: DALY
words.description: disability-adjusted life year (UN); one lost year of healthy life

words.name: HTP
words.description: Happy Time Point; DALY extended to Maslow levels 1-2, impact-weighted reach

words.name: RSI
words.description: recursive self-improvement: AI that builds the next, better AI generation - distinct from intra-task self-verification

words.name: verification
words.description: intra-task self-checking: extra test/correction code within a single task

triples.name: overstimulation gap
triples.description: orders-of-magnitude gap in intensity x vulnerability between the neonatal-mouse model dose and adult status-quo media use

triples.name: flip probability
triples.description: probability that the measurement actually flips the decision sign

triples.name: avoided wrong decision
triples.description: harm of the wrong decision that the measurement avoids

triples.name: coercion structure
triples.description: lever: mandatory justification before voting; forces checking instead of inviting it

triples.name: identity group
triples.description: lever: peer norm makes checking a status marker

triples.name: direct incentive
triples.description: lever: ranking / reputation / money reward for rare-but-right

triples.name: friction reduction
triples.description: lever: remove distraction; acts on opportunity not motivation - the petition's mechanism

triples.name: lever score
triples.description: effect x reach product, ordinal, for ranking the levers only

sources.name: Christakis 2012 mouse overstimulation
sources.description: Christakis DA, Ramirez JSB, Ramirez JM (2012). Overstimulation of newborn mice leads to behavioral differences and deficits in cognitive performance. Scientific Reports 2:546. p10 mice, 70 dB audio + flashing lights, 6 h/day, 42 days; overstimulated mice performed significantly worse on Light-Dark Latency, Elevated Plus Maze, Novel Object Recognition and Barnes Maze.

sources.name: Anthropic RSI warning (heise 2026)
sources.description: heise online: Skynet-Szenario - Anthropic warnt vor KI, die sich selbst entwickelt. Source of the RSI definition used to separate recursive self-improvement from intra-task self-verification.

sources.name: WHO cost-effectiveness threshold
sources.description: Health-economic convention: an intervention's cost per DALY averted, here a set ~50000 CHF/DALY threshold used only to convert study cost into HTP.

formulas.name: status quo harm weight formula
formulas.expression: "status quo harm weight" = "model harm weight" / "overstimulation gap"

formulas.name: status quo harm per person formula
formulas.expression: "status quo harm per person" = "status quo harm weight" * "exposure duration"

formulas.name: status quo total harm formula
formulas.expression: "status quo total harm" = "status quo harm per person" * "population"

formulas.name: rollback net balance formula
formulas.expression: "rollback net balance" = "status quo total harm" - "rollback cost"

formulas.name: value of information ratio formula
formulas.expression: "value of information ratio" = "value of information" / "study cost"

calc-validation.note: CONTRA the status-quo-is-harmful thesis: lower bound, gap factor 100. With gap 10 the result would be 0.02.

calc-validation.formula: status quo harm per person formula

calc-validation.note: PRO the thesis: even at the conservative lower bound the diffuse mass harm is ~6.4M HTP, because reach is huge while per-capita weight is tiny. This is the strength of the HTP logic - it makes sub-threshold mass harm visible.
CONTRA naive rollback: primum non nocere requires the intervention's own cost (~4.2M HTP) be counted against the harm it averts.

KEY RESULT - sign uncertain: at lower bound (gap 100) net = 6.4M - 4.2M = 2.2M, near a wash given the crude inputs. At upper bound (gap 10) status-quo harm = 64M, net = 59.8M, a clear rollback win. The whole decision hinges on the single un-measured gap factor.
Harm of measuring: ~70 HTP of foregone alternative use of the research budget. Observation study, so participant harm ~0.

PRO measuring first: benefit ~2.5M HTP vs cost ~70 HTP gives ~35700:1 (the discussion's spoken ~25000:1 was a looser estimate; recomputed here). Unlike rollback and petition, the SIGN of measuring is robust. Caveat: holds only if the study design can actually flip the sign - a confounded observational study flips nothing and the ratio collapses.

Documented dose of the mouse model: 6 h/day x 42 days = 252 h at 70 dB + flashing light, neonatal. The argument the source does NOT support: extrapolating this non-normative neonatal extreme to adult status-quo media use. Datapoint, not keystone.
Readiness lever ranking (effect x reach, ordinal). Coercion/structure highest - forces checking, reaches the unwilling.

CONTRA the petition: friction reduction (the petition's mechanism) ranks last - it acts on opportunity, not motivation, reaching only the already-willing. Three stronger levers are already inside zukunft.com.
